prediction.reasoning: The prediction is based on a model that assumes PNC's stock price follows a geometric Brownian motion. Starting from the current price of $220.77, an annualized volatility of 25% and an annualized drift (expected return) of 8% were used. The point estimate reflects a modest positive drift over the 18-day period. The confidence intervals are derived from the log-normal distribution inherent in this model, indicating the range within which the price is expected to fall at different confidence levels. The increasing width of the intervals reflects the escalating uncertainty with higher confidence requirements due to the inherent volatility of financial stocks over short time horizons.
